---
title: "About this quiz"
---

## What this is

This is a **self-assessment quiz** on open science and scholarly publishing,
aimed primarily at scholarly communications librarians, but useful to
researchers, research infrastructure providers, and technologists who work in
this space. It is a learning tool, not a test with stakes: nothing is recorded,
and you can retake it as often as you like.

## How it works

1. Choose one **difficulty level** and one or more **topic areas**.
2. Answer the matching questions one at a time.
3. After each answer you see whether you were right, an explanation, and
   (where available) a link to authoritative further reading.
4. At the end you get a score and, if you picked more than one area, a
   breakdown by area.

Questions live in a single CSV file in the project repository
(`data/questions.csv`) and are loaded in your browser when the page opens. The
site is fully static — there is no server, database, or account.

## Difficulty levels

| Level | Focus |
|---|---|
| **Beginner** | Foundational definitions — "what is X" |
| **Intermediate** | How things work and how pieces connect |
| **Expert** | Nuanced distinctions, standards and policy detail, comparing similar tools |
| **Master** | Edge cases, governance and history, cross-system interoperability, and debates in the field |

## Topic areas

- Persistent Identifiers (DOI, ORCID, ROR, RRID, IGSN, ARK, …)
- Research Data Management (FAIR, CARE, DMPs, repositories)
- Open Access (models, licensing, rights retention)
- Research Assessment & Metrics (DORA, CoARA, Leiden Manifesto, JIF, h-index)
- Research Integrity (misconduct definitions, COPE, retractions)
- Reproducibility & Open Methods (preregistration, Registered Reports, TOP)
- Scholarly Infrastructure & Organizations (Crossref, DataCite, OpenAlex, POSI, …)
- Preprints & Peer Review (arXiv, bioRxiv, open review, overlay journals)
- Open Source & Software (software citation, JOSS, Zenodo archiving)
- Policy & Funder Mandates (Plan S, the 2022 OSTP memo, UNESCO, NIH DMS)

## Accuracy and sources

Questions were written using the referenced organisations' own documentation,
FAQs, and policy pages rather than from memory, because terminology, governance,
and scope in this area change over time. Each question carries a `source_url`
pointing to the documentation consulted. A small number of questions are flagged
internally (in the CSV's `review_note` column) where a fact should be
re-verified before wider publication — for example, figures that grow over time
or terms with contested definitions. **If you spot an error, please open an
issue or edit `data/questions.csv` and submit a pull request.**

## Privacy

There is no analytics, no login, and no storage of your answers. Refreshing the
page resets everything.
